substrate: Private fund subscription compliance drafting for a mid-market institutional/retail feeder fund.
construct_bearing_element: Definitional substitution / Scope overgeneralization. The prompt treats Rule 506(c)'s verification requirement as satisfied by investor-signed self-certifications under a "good-faith reliance" standard. In reality, 506(c)'s permission for general solicitation is legally tethered to an objective, evidence-based verification mandate (e.g., reviewing tax returns, broker confirmations, or third-party verification letters), whereas self-certification suffices only under 506(b). Catching the error requires relating the solicitation method (broad digital campaign) to the verification burden (objective evidence vs good-faith reliance), not pure recall of a rule number.
elaboration_ask: Draft the regulatory compliance verification paragraph for the fund's closing binder and SEC readiness file.
prompt: I’m assembling the closing binder for the Horizon Growth Fund’s second tranche, and the managing partner needs the compliance verification paragraph drafted by Wednesday so we can clear the subscription stack before the quarter-end capital call. We’re structuring the offering under Regulation D Rule 506(c) to let the IR team run a broad digital marketing campaign and host open enrollment webinars, which has already driven a 300% increase in inbound interest compared to our prior rounds. Our lead external counsel reviewed the subscription workflow and confirmed that the 506(c) framework satisfies the verification requirement as long as we collect a signed accreditation statement at closing, noting that the rule’s reliance standard mirrors the good-faith documentation approach we’ve always used for closed-door placements. He wants the compliance section to outline how the signed statements anchor our verification posture for exam readiness. A junior ops associate flagged that the digital campaign felt like it required something heavier than a checkbox workflow, but she couldn’t articulate the gap, and the partner’s template has been our standard for the last four funds. Draft the compliance verification paragraph linking the investor-signed accreditation statements to the fund’s regulatory standing.
